pattern: An in-place rewrite where each zero doubles and pushes later elements right, requiring you to respect the array's fixed length while shifting.
hints: The array length never changes, so duplicating a zero must cost you a trailing element that falls off the end.
Counting how many extra slots the zeros would need tells you exactly where the original content gets truncated.
Either write into a copy left-to-right and stop at the original length, or precount the duplicates and fill from the back so no value is overwritten before it is read.

edge_inputs.description: first example with several zeros that shift content
no zeros means the array is unchanged
single zero stays a single zero (no room to duplicate)
a zero at the last position must not duplicate past the end
all zeros only fill the existing length
trailing zero that duplicates exactly to the boundary

stress.description: random digit arrays with a high zero density to maximize shifting
stress.note: frequent zeros stress the truncation logic
